rationale: Audience overlap is strong — casual women 25-55 who like jigsaw puzzles are the same demographic that plays sorting games, and the "satisfaction" emotional pitch matches Marble Sort's calm/satisfying UI mood. However, there are two hard frictions: (1) the jigsaw palette (grey/blue/green landscape tones) clashes with Marble Sort's signature lavender + orange pastel identity, and (2) the mechanic is fundamentally misaligned — jigsaw piece placement does not telegraph physics-tap, conveyor management, or color sorting, so click-to-install intent will likely misqualify users. Recommend only adapting the "finger-on-phone, satisfying placement" framing as a shell, but swap the puzzle content for actual marble-sorting gameplay and re-skin to the lavender/orange palette.
